data.narrative:
World Labs, a Series B AI & Software company founded by Fei-Fei Li, Justin Johnson, Christoph Lassner, and Ben Mildenhall, carries a current post-money valuation of $5.0B against $1.23B+ in total raised. The peer universe comprises 9 comparable companies—1 public (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and 8 private entities operating in the same sector and stage, including Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, Nominal, and Photoroom.

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement to the $5.0B base, implying a valuation of $9.5B. Peer-median methodology across four same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median of $3.3B, with peer valuations ranging from $500M (Photoroom) to $32.0B (Safe Superintelligence). The wide spread—particularly SSI's outlier position—reflects material dispersion in this cohort.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $2.8B (low) to $10.9B (high), with a median of $6.4B. The current $5.0B valuation sits below the median estimate, positioning it defensibly within the lower-middle quartile. Key uncertainties include the appropriate weighting of Safe Superintelligence as a comp given its significant valuation premium, and whether stage-progression multiples remain stable in a volatile frontier-AI funding environment. Revenue visibility and customer concentration remain unspecified and warrant further diligence.
